item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli Military Orders Evacuation of 11 Southern Lebanon Towns
item.description: The Israeli military issued an urgent evacuation order for residents of 11 towns and villages in southern Lebanon, telling them to move at least 1,000 meters away from their homes amid ongoing operations against Hezbollah.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany Calls US Troop Withdrawal Foreseeable as Trump Threatens More Cuts
item.description: Germanys defence minister described the US decision to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany as foreseeable, while Donald Trump warned he may reduce forces further. NATO and Republican lawmakers expressed concern about the move.

item.title: 🇱🇧 IDF orders evacuations for 11 southern Lebanon towns amid anti-Hezbollah operations
item.description: The Israeli military issued urgent evacuation warnings to residents of 11 towns and villages in southern Lebanon, telling them to move at least 1,000 meters away as operations against Hezbollah continue despite a fragile ceasefire.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇵🇭🇨🇳 US-Philippines Deploy Anti-Ship Missile System Near Taiwan in War Games
item.description: Philippine and US forces showcased the NMESIS anti-ship missile system in Batanes province near Taiwan during annual military exercises, as regional tensions over the self-governed island continue to escalate.

item.title: 🛢️💰 OPEC+ Agrees Symbolic Oil Output Hike Despite Hormuz Closure
item.description: OPEC+ agreed in principle to a small oil output quota hike, though the increase will remain largely symbolic until shipping through the Strait of Hormuz can resume amid the ongoing Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇮🇷💸 Millions of Jobs Lost as Iran Battles "Operation Economic Fury" Fallout
item.description: A fragile ceasefire may have paused the US-Israeli war on Iran, but the economic cost is devastating Tehran, with millions of jobs lost and crippling effects on daily life from the sustained military and economic pressure campaign.

item.title: 🇮🇳🇦🇪 Modi's UAE visit signals new era for India's Middle East policy
item.description: Prime Minister Modi's upcoming UAE stopover represents a strategic opportunity for India to establish a key pivot in the Persian Gulf, similar to Singapore's role in Southeast Asia. The visit underscores the Emirates importance in India's regional calculations.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇩🇪 US Troop Drawdown from Germany Signals Shift in European Defense Responsibilities
item.description: Germany's defense minister says the US withdrawal of 5,000 troops should spur Europe to take greater defense responsibility, as Trump-Pentagon tensions with Europe mount over Iran war and tariffs. Trump signaled cuts could go "a lot further."

item.title: 🇮🇷⚔️ Iran Standoff Could Leave Trump Worse Off Than Before War Began
item.description: Reuters analysis finds the US-Iran conflict has failed to deliver a decisive military or diplomatic victory, with Tehran's blockade strategy and Trump's stalled negotiations leaving both sides in an uneasy stalemate.

item.title: ⛪🇮🇷 Jailed Iranian Nobel Laureate Narges Mohammadi in Hospital After Cardiac Crisis
item.description: Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi's health has "deteriorated seriously" in Iranian custody, with the Norwegian Nobel Committee calling for her release to receive proper medical care.

item.title: 🇹🇲🌍 Reclusive Turkmenistan Shows Signs of Cautiously Opening to the World
item.description: The isolated Central Asian nation is taking tentative steps toward economic modernization and international engagement, with new tech startups and easing restrictions signaling a shift from decades of isolation.

item.title: 🇸🇦🇹🇷🇦🇪 Saudi Arabia, Turkey and UAE propose new trade corridors bypassing Hormuz
item.description: Middle Eastern governments are reviving decades-old pipeline proposals and drawing up new rail-sea transport corridors as a structural shift away from dependence on vulnerable Persian Gulf shipping routes. The plan would route cargo from UAE and Oman ports through Saudi Arabia to Jordan, then to Egypt's Suez or Syrian ports.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 Iran says "ball in US court" on diplomacy as senior military expects renewed conflict
item.description: Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister said Tehran is prepared for both diplomacy or continued confrontation, while a senior Iranian military commander warned that renewed conflict with the US is "likely." The statements come as Trump reviews a new 14-point Iranian peace proposal.

item.title: 🇹🇼 China Labels Taiwan President a Rat After Failed Diplomatic Blockade
item.description: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te arrived in Eswatini for a surprise visit after China pressured multiple African nations to deny overflight permits. Beijing condemned Lai as a rat, highlighting escalating cross-strait tensions.

item.title: 🚢 US Warns Shippers of Sanctions for Paying Iran Strait of Hormuz Tolls
item.description: The United States has warned global shipping companies they could face sanctions if they pay Iran for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, escalating the financial dimension of the Iran crisis.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Trump Auto Tariffs Could Cost Germany Nearly $18 Billion in Output
item.description: A German economic institute estimates President Trumps tariff hike on EU cars and trucks could cost Germany nearly 15 billion euros in lost output, straining transatlantic trade relations.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan to Receive Russian Sakhalin-2 Oil Cargo as Iran War Disrupts Gulf Supplies
item.description: Japanese refinery Taiyo Oil will receive a crude oil cargo from Russias Sakhalin-2 project next week, as Japan seeks alternative petroleum sources following the US-Iran conflict that limited Gulf supplies.

item.title: 🇰🇭 Cambodia Upholds Treason Conviction of Opposition Leader
item.description: A Cambodian appeals court upheld the treason conviction and 27-year prison sentence for opposition leader Kem Sokha, drawing concern from the US State Department and UN rights chief.

item.title: ⚔️ Iran War Reaches Two-Month Mark With No Clear Victory
item.description: Two months into the US-Iran conflict, fighting has been suspended but a definitive end remains elusive. Washington lacks clear strategic gains as oil prices stay elevated and diplomatic solutions stall.

item.title: 💰 Iran War Drives US Inflation Expectations Higher
item.description: US headline and core inflation are heading back above 3% as the Iran war-related oil shock continues to affect prices, building inflation expectations for the year ahead.

item.title: ⚓ US Navy Turns to AI Firm to Counter Iranian Mines in Strait
item.description: The US Navy has contracted AI company Domino to develop countermeasures against Iranian naval mines as the Strait of Hormuz crisis continues. The move reflects growing concerns about Iranian mining capabilities disrupting global oil shipments.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Develops SQUID Technology to Detect US Nuclear Submarines
item.description: Chinese scientists have made advances in gravity-detecting SQUID technology that could make it easier to track US nuclear submarines. The development could shift the strategic balance in underwater warfare.

item.title: 🇨🇺 Cuba Condemns New US Sanctions as "Collective Punishment"
item.description: Cuba denounced fresh US sanctions as illegal and abusive, coming on top of a US oil blockade causing widespread blackouts and fuel shortages. The new measures escalate economic pressure on the island nation.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Mali Investigates Soldiers for Role in Military Base Attacks
item.description: Five army personnel including three active-duty soldiers identified as suspects in last week coordinated attacks on military bases. The investigation raises concerns about insider threats amid ongoing security challenges.

item.title: 🇿🇲 Zambia Cancels World Largest Human Rights Summit Days Before Start
item.description: Government blocked RightsCon 2026 conference saying it did not align with national values. The cancellation of the world largest human rights and tech summit raises concerns about democratic freedoms.
